address: The George Inn, Leek Rd, Waterhouses, Staffordshire ST10 3HW
lead_profile_text: Moving to US; 6-bed large group property (The George Inn, Waterhouses). Managed remotely. No direct comparable in current Stayful portfolio. Sister has Cornwall properties — pipeline opportunity. First large-format group property in Stayful pipeline.
emotional_profile: Analytically engaged and time-pressured — real US departure deadline creates genuine urgency. Remote management reassurance is primary emotional need. Thinking expansively (sister Cornwall properties).
green_light_types: Time pressure (US departure end of next week)
Sister Cornwall pipeline mentioned — expansive engagement
green_light_trigger_phrases: I need to decide before I go back
Let's have a call Monday 1 June to review the materials
I'll send the photos by the weekend
My sister has properties in Cornwall

top_objections.text: Managing the property remotely — returning to US and cannot be on-site
top_objections.zac_response: Slack channel with dedicated team, monthly statements 1st-5th, quarterly inspections — full visibility without physical presence needed

top_objections.text: I want to understand the costs more — full cost transparency required
top_objections.zac_response: Full cost breakdown: guest pays cleaning (~£200/stay), owner covers mortgage, insurance, utilities, council tax, annual gas safety. Maintenance auto-threshold £300/month

top_objections.text: I'd like more control over pricing — wants oversight of dynamic pricing decisions
top_objections.zac_response: Dynamic pricing reviewed weekly with manual oversight — owner can input preferences and floor rates

top_objections.text: Not being kept informed — key emotional need around communication and visibility
top_objections.zac_response: Dedicated Slack channel, monthly statements, quarterly performance reviews address this directly

top_objections.text: The fee structure — analytical concern, wants to know exactly what's included
top_objections.zac_response: 13% + VAT (discounted from 15%) covers end-to-end: cleaning coordination, maintenance, guest vetting, platform management, reporting

top_objections.text: Why doesn't Stayful use VRBO given its off-season performance and improved pricing tools vs Airbnb?
top_objections.zac_response: Reviews concentrated on Airbnb and Booking.com to drive platform ranking — VRBO dilutes review base

top_objections.text: Hot tub — questioned whether to add one
top_objections.zac_response: Mutually agreed to defer — assess property performance first before adding maintenance complexity

top_questions.text: What is the forecasted net revenue for the property?
top_questions.answer: ~£90,000 annually net. Comparable large-format properties earning £120-150k. Average nightly rate £952. Low occupancy expected due to large capacity group bookings. Peak season (August) doubles February revenue.

top_questions.text: What is the management fee and what does it include?
top_questions.answer: Standard 15% + VAT. Reduced to 13% + VAT if signed by 14 June 2026. Includes: cleaning, maintenance coordination, guest vetting, platform management, monthly statements, quarterly inspections.

top_questions.text: How does guest vetting and property protection work for a high-value property?
top_questions.answer: £500 security deposit (elevated for property value), ID checks with damage history screening, £100k damage cover per stay.

top_questions.text: What is the timeline from contract signing to going live?
top_questions.answer: 3 weeks from contract to go-live — includes photographer, cleaner inspection, snagging list.

top_questions.text: How are maintenance and repairs handled remotely?
top_questions.answer: Automatic approval for work up to £300/month; above that requires owner sign-off. Coordinated through dedicated team. Owner notified via Slack.

top_questions.text: What platforms will the property be listed on?
top_questions.answer: Airbnb, Booking.com, and Stayful direct booking site. VRBO not currently used — reviews concentrated on main channels to build ranking.

top_questions.text: Should we add a hot tub?
top_questions.answer: Mutually agreed to defer — wait to assess performance and occupancy before introducing hot tub maintenance complexity.

what_moved_them: 6 years trading and 80 properties — scale gave credibility
£500 security deposit noted positively — showed Stayful takes property protection seriously for high-value property
Quarterly inspections — reassured Helen on property oversight from a distance
Monthly statements on 1st-5th — very specific timeline gave confidence
3 weeks contract to live — shorter timeline than expected
Sister's Cornwall properties mentioned — Helen thinking expansively and genuinely engaged
what_fell_flat: VRBO exclusion not fully resolved — Helen questioned this given its off-season performance
Pricing control — dynamic pricing model left residual concern about autonomy

conversion_gaps.gap: No worst-case monthly floor provided for large-format group property
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Provide seasonal monthly range (floor and ceiling) for 6-bed group properties at follow-up call on June 1
Prepare concise explanation of why review concentration on Airbnb and Booking.com outperforms VRBO distribution at this stage

conversion_gaps.gap: Pricing oversight mechanism not fully explained
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Show Helen the owner pricing input process — she can set floor rates and flag preferences

offer_framing: 13% + VAT (vs standard 15%) — saving 2 percentage points — if agreement signed by 14 June 2026, aligned with Helen's US departure timeline.
zac_income_intro: Comparable properties earning £120-150k annually. Conservative forecast for Helen's property is ~£90,000 net based on large-format group booking profile. Average nightly rate £952 with seasonal variation.
zac_transition_phrases: The reason we focus on Airbnb and Booking.com is to concentrate reviews — spreading across VRBO dilutes that
Because you're moving to the US, the Slack channel becomes your eyes and ears on the property
3 weeks from contract to go-live — that fits well before your departure
zac_reassurance_phrases: You won't need to be physically present for any of this
Monthly statements land 1st to 5th every month — you'll always know exactly what's happening
Quarterly inspection calls mean nothing falls through the cracks
We hold a £500 deposit specifically because of the property's value — higher than our standard
zac_analogies: The Slack channel is like having a property manager in your pocket — wherever you are in the US
